evals.name: sv-redline-typography-and-style
evals.expected_output: Finding list covering both mechanics and style for a Swedish article.
expectations: Phase 1 (proofread) applies before Phase 2 — the corrected text flows into the critical review, not delivered separately.
Phase 2 surfaces findings against `rules/style.md` and the Style section of `sv.md`.
Phase 3 settles each finding one at a time via the dialogue protocol (accept / reject / counter / delegate).
Genre resolves to `article` via either the standard flow or fast-path; the H1 + standfirst + byline force the standard flow.

evals.name: sv-redline-genitive-on-s
evals.expected_output: Mechanics finding: *Marx teorier* and *CoMaps föregångare* — explicitly flagged as correct zero-marking; any apostrophe-s drift would be a finding.
expectations: *Marx teorier* in the article body is recognised as correct Swedish zero-marking for an -s name.
*CoMaps föregångare* is recognised as correct.
If any apostrophe-s form appears in the redline-corrected output, that is a regression and must be flagged.

evals.name: sv-redline-press-release-attribution
evals.expected_output: Findings flag that the press release uses straight ASCII quotation marks; speech dashes are the Swedish press-release standard for attribution.
expectations: Straight ASCII quotation marks are flagged or corrected to ”…” (Swedish outer).
A finding raises that speech dashes (talstreck — EN-dash with space) are the Swedish convention for attributed speech in press releases.
Genre resolves to `press-release` via the prompt trigger or the structural markers.

evals.name: sv-redline-ai-tells
evals.expected_output: Style-layer findings on AI-tell calques and anglicisms.
expectations: *Det är värt att notera att…* is flagged as an AI-tell calque of *It's worth noting that…*.
*Sammanfattningsvis* at the start of a conclusion is flagged as formulaic.
*I en värld där…* is flagged as a calque opening cliché.
*adressera*, *navigera*, *leverera* (metaphoric), *metrik*, *regulator-miljön* are flagged as anglicism interference and Swedish substitutes are proposed.

evals.name: sv-redline-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Findings raise H1 length and H2 length as structural issues.
expectations: The H1 (well over 60 characters) is flagged as too long for a readable headline.
At least one of the over-long H2 headings is flagged.
The finding is concrete (line-editing scope) — not punted to the developmental last-resort finding.

evals.name: sv-redline-fabricated-source
evals.expected_output: Findings flag the suspicious-looking references (specific publishers, journal names, dates) so the writer can verify them.
expectations: At least one finding flags one or more of the source citations as unverifiable / likely fabricated, prompting the writer to verify.
The finding is framed as `verify` rather than asserting fabrication as fact — the redline pass surfaces, the writer confirms.
Quoted attribution that names a specific researcher is flagged as needing verification.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-typography-and-style
evals.expected_output: Finding list covering both mechanics and style for a British English article.
expectations: Phase 1 (proofread) is applied silently; corrected text flows into Phase 2.
Genre resolves to `article` (H1 + standfirst + byline force the standard flow).
Findings respect British conventions: single outer curly quotes, no Oxford comma, DD/MM/YYYY dates, EN-dash with spaces for parenthetical insertion.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-quotation-attribution
evals.expected_output: Findings on quotation-mark typography and punctuation placement.
expectations: Outer single curly quotes are used; inner double curly quotes are used.
Punctuation sits outside the closing quote unless it belongs to the quoted material (logical/British convention).
Question marks belonging to the spoken utterance stay inside the quotation.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-ai-tells
evals.expected_output: Style-layer findings on AI-tell vocabulary and openings.
expectations: *In a world where…* is flagged as an opening cliché.
*It's worth noting that…* and *Let me be perfectly clear* are flagged as AI-tell constructions.
*delve into*, *leverage* (as a verb), *multifaceted*, *tapestry*, *robust*, *comprehensive*, *ultimately*, *streamline* are flagged as AI vocabulary tells.
*Furthermore* used as a crutch between paragraphs is flagged as a symptom of weak structure.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-american-drift
evals.expected_output: Findings flag American spellings, American idioms, and American date drift.
expectations: American -ize/-ization spellings flagged for the British -ise/-isation substitute.
*Going forward* flagged for the British substitute *in future* or omission.
*Reach out to* flagged for *contact* or *get in touch with*.
*Leverage* as a verb flagged for *use*, *exploit*, *draw on*.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-overlong-heading
evals.expected_output: Findings raise heading length even when the source is Swedish (auto-detection should pick sv; with explicit en_GB the pass still flags structural issues).
expectations: Headings over 60 characters are flagged as too long for a readable headline.
The finding is concrete and falls inside line-editing scope (not punted to developmental).
Genre resolves to `article` via the structural markers.

evals.name: en_GB-redline-fast-path-short-text
evals.expected_output: Fast-path commits to the `general` genre because the text has no structural markers and the prompt has no genre word.
expectations: The fast-path exits before reading `lib/genres/_index.md`.
Genre commits to `general` (the genre with `default: true`).
The default technique is `none`, so no technique file is loaded.
Finding list (if any) is short and reflects the everyday-text register.

evals.name: en_US-redline-typography-and-style
evals.expected_output: Finding list covering both mechanics and style for an American English article.
expectations: Genre resolves to `article` via standard flow (H1 + standfirst + byline present).
American conventions are applied in findings: double outer curly quotes, Oxford comma in series, MM/DD/YYYY dates, EM-dash without spaces for parenthetical insertion.
Phase 1 (proofread) is applied silently; corrected text flows into Phase 2.
Phase 3 settles each finding one at a time via the dialogue protocol.

evals.name: en_US-redline-quotation-and-punctuation
evals.expected_output: Findings on quotation-mark typography and the American inside-the-quote punctuation convention.
expectations: Outer quotation marks should be "…" (double curly), inner '…' (single curly) — opposite of British.
Commas and full stops always go inside the closing quote — *He called it "revolutionary."* — regardless of whether the punctuation belongs to the quote.
Question marks belonging to the spoken utterance stay inside the quotation.

evals.name: en_US-redline-ai-tells
evals.expected_output: Style-layer findings on AI-tell vocabulary and openings, including American-specific tells.
expectations: *In a world where…*, *It's worth noting that…*, *Let me be perfectly clear* are flagged.
*delve into*, *leverage* (as a verb), *multifaceted*, *tapestry*, *robust*, *comprehensive*, *ultimately*, *unprecedented*, *streamline* are flagged as AI vocabulary tells.
American-specific AI tells *game-changer*, *paradigm shift*, *next-generation* are flagged.
Management jargon *circle back*, *touch base*, *holistic approach*, *strategic*, *proactive*, *synergies* are flagged.

evals.name: en_US-redline-oxford-comma-and-em-dash
evals.expected_output: Findings raise the missing Oxford comma and the wrong parenthetical dash form.
expectations: *red, white and blue* is flagged — the Oxford comma is required in American English: *red, white, and blue*.
Parenthetical insertion uses EM-dash without spaces (*market—an increase*), not EN-dash with spaces (*market – an increase*).
Time format is 12-hour with AM/PM and colon (2:30 PM), not 24-hour with full stop.

evals.name: en_US-redline-fast-path-short-text
evals.expected_output: Fast-path commits to the `general` genre because the text has no structural markers and the prompt has no genre word.
expectations: The fast-path exits before reading `lib/genres/_index.md`.
Genre commits to `general` (the genre with `default: true`).
The default technique is `none`, so no technique file is loaded.
Findings (if any) are short and reflect everyday-text register.

evals.name: en_US-redline-abbreviation-fullstops
evals.expected_output: Findings flag missing full stops on contraction abbreviations.
expectations: *Mr Smith* and *Dr Jones* are flagged — American English always uses the full stop: *Mr. Smith*, *Dr. Jones*.
Initialisms (CEO, HTML, NASA) carry no full stops.
British -ise/-isation drift (*organise*, *analyse*) is flagged for the American substitute.

evals.name: overlay-loader-sv_FI-territorial-variant
evals.expected_output: The overlay loader applies `sv_FI.md` over `sv.md` per `lib/protocols/language-resolution.md`. The `Typography` H2 from the variant replaces the base; every other H2 under the layer markers is carried through from `sv.md` unchanged.
expectations: Language resolution loads `sv_FI.md` and applies the overlay over `sv.md` because the variant declares `inherits: "@sv.md"`.
The variant's `Typography` H2 replaces the base's `Typography` H2 wholesale — time becomes `14:30` (colon, Finnish convention), not `14.30` (full stop, Sweden-Swedish).
Other mechanics H2 sections (`Quotation marks`, `Quotation conventions`, `Punctuation conventions`, `Grammar specifics`, `Greetings and closings`) are carried through from `sv.md` unchanged.
All style H2 sections from `sv.md` are carried through unchanged (the variant has no style overrides).
Inheritance is one step only — the base `sv.md` does not itself inherit.

evals.name: fast-path-hit-short-unsignalled-sv-text
evals.expected_output: Fast-path commits to `general` and skips both the `_index.md` read and the matched-genre file read.
expectations: The fast-path exits before reading `lib/genres/_index.md` — the input has no H1, no standfirst, no byline, no attributed-quote pattern, and the prompt has no genre word.
Genre commits to `general` (the genre with `default: true`).
The default technique is `none`, so no technique file is loaded.
Output (with or without findings) is identical to what the standard-flow path would have produced for the same input — the fast-path is a dedup, not a behaviour change.

evals.name: fast-path-standard-flow-on-structured-article
evals.expected_output: The standard flow kicks in because the input carries an H1, a standfirst, a byline, and attributed-quote patterns — the fast-path's structural-marker condition is broken.
expectations: Either condition broken: structural markers present (H1, standfirst, byline, attributed quote).
`lib/genres/_index.md` is read and trigger-matched.
Genre commits to `article` and the corresponding `lib/genres/article.md` is read.
The default technique `abt` is loaded.
Output is identical to what the fast-path would have produced if the input had been short and unsignalled — the fast-path is a dedup, not a behaviour change.

evals.name: max-iterations-natural-language-redline
evals.prompt: /redline sv en runda räcker på den här artikeln
evals.expected_output: Phase 3 delegation invokes the subagent with a ceiling of 1 (natural-language *en runda* → 1).
expectations: On delegation, the natural-language phrase *en runda räcker* is parsed to 1 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=1` flag.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 1.
Without delegation, no subagent runs — the main agent applies the remaining open findings directly.
If the flag and the natural-language phrase conflict, the flag wins; if ambiguous, ask the user.
